_comment: jurisdiction-desk composes the ADVISORY jurisdictional-overlay atoms. All attribute and cached-geometry evaluation runs offline in tools/geo_overlay.py (EPSG:4326); live FEMA flood queries run only when jurisdictional_overlay_live is on (tools/geo_fetch.py). Every output carries the advisory-not-legal-determination boundary; genuine legal conflicts return human_review_required, never an auto-decision. Optional and default off. Advisory only: no code text (construction-desk), no project planning (project-builder), no legal/permitting/engineering advice.
description: Advisory jurisdictional overlays for a Florida or North Carolina project location: which location-based rules apply (flood, historic, HVHZ, steep-slope, watershed, SB 4D) and, on conflict, which governs.

steps.input_from: the project location (lon/lat, EPSG:4326) + known building facts
steps.condition: always; resolves which advisory overlays apply, each cited

steps.input_from: two applicable overlays from step 1 that address the same feature
steps.condition: only when two applicable overlays collide; returns the governing rule or human_review_required with a PROV audit

steps.input_from: the assembled overlays + any conflict decision from steps 1 to 2
steps.condition: always; enforces the advisory boundary, citations, no fabrication, and formatting before handoff

notes: overlay-resolve and conflict-check are directly callable by the user. Geometry overlays needing a live boundary return a config gap when jurisdictional_overlay_live is off; nothing is fetched.
